title: juncus bufonius subsp. fasciculatus
intro: Juncus bufonius subsp. fasciculatus is a subspecies of the greater marsh rush plant, which is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Juncaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Juncus bufonius subsp. fasciculatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
